title: AIM Review Output Schema (aim-review-v1.0)
description: Output schema for the AIM Reviewer stage (pipeline/engine/review_base.py → review-YYYY-MM-DD.json + review-latest.json). Sprint 3 Step 2b role insertion (AD-2026-04-23i); per-patch verdicts added by AD-2026-04-26X (G4+G5). The Reviewer sits between the Interpreter and the Composer: it reads interpret-latest.json, runs R1–R7 deterministic rule-engine checks (disposition-aware per design §3.3), emits a brief-level 5-value verdict, and emits one per-patch verdict per interpret.proposed_patches[] entry. LLM call produces advisory_notes[] only; verdict + checks + claim_flags + patch_verdicts are all rule-engine-deterministic and not LLM-overridable.

reviewer_model.description: Model used for the advisory_notes LLM call (sonar-pro default). Null when REVIEWER_NO_LLM=1 or under error_cycle short-circuit. Verdict is independent of LLM output.

reviewed_from_file.description: Filename of the interpret output this review consumed. Typically 'interpret-latest.json' or 'interpret-YYYY-MM-DD.json'.

interpret_meta.description: Cherry-picked mirror of the upstream Interpreter _meta fields that the Composer will care about. Engine-stamped.

reviewer_flags.description: Engine-side flags (not LLM-emitted). E.g. 'rule_engine_only' when LLM skipped.

reviewer_error.description: Engine-stamped. True only when the LLM advisory_notes call failed. Does NOT affect verdict (rule engine is independent).

independence_mechanism.description: Sprint 3 Step 4a Risk 3 fix (AD-2026-04-23l). Audit-documentation field naming the mechanisms that make the Reviewer's verdict independent of the Interpreter. Engine-stamped verbatim from metadata.yml stages.reviewer.independence_mechanism. Read by P2 adjudication (independent review criterion).

distinct_model_family.description: Names the distinct LLM families used by Reviewer vs Interpreter. Concrete evidence that the advisory_notes call is not the same model that produced the interpret output.

first_principles_source_re_read.description: True when the Reviewer prompt instructs first-principles re-reading of source evidence rather than deferring to the Interpreter's framing.

verdict.description: 5-value verdict (design §3.2, D2). Set by rule engine in pipeline/engine/review_base.py.derive_verdict(); NOT LLM-overridable.

verdict_reason.description: Short prose reason from the rule engine. Suitable for logs and composer_flags lineage.

checks.description: Rule-engine results for R1–R7 per design §3.3. Ordered by check_id.

disposition_aware_skip.description: True when the check was skipped because of the upstream cycle_disposition (expected and healthy).

claim_flags.description: Per-claim findings from the rule engine (R3 shape integrity + R4 evidence completeness). Never LLM-emitted.

advisory_notes.description: Optional prose observations from the LLM call. Composer may reflect but not claim as facts. Empty under REVIEWER_NO_LLM=1 or rule_engine_only mode.

patch_verdicts.description: G4+G5 (AD-2026-04-26X). Per-patch verdicts derived by pipeline/engine/review_base.py.derive_patch_verdicts() against interpret.proposed_patches[]. One entry per proposed patch. Empty array when interpret carried no patches. Verdict vocabulary is engine-fixed per pipeline-purpose-and-roles.md §3.5 + Appendix C; the rule sets that derive these verdicts are AGM-internal (R1–R7 per ENGINE-RULES §1j). LLM never overrides — verdicts are rule-engine-deterministic.

patch_id.description: Stable patch ID from interpret.proposed_patches[].patch_id. If absent, Reviewer stamps `_unstamped_<idx>`.

verdict.description: Per-patch verdict (canonical §3.5 + Appendix C). Loop-back verdicts are next-cycle deferral payloads, not mid-cycle control flow (§4.4 as clarified by AD-2026-04-26X).

verdict_reason.description: Short prose reason from the rule engine. Names the rule(s) or condition that produced the verdict.

rules_passed.description: Cycle-level R-rule check_ids that PASS. May be empty under disposition short-circuits.

rules_failed.description: Cycle-level R-rule check_ids that FAIL. Drives reject (R1–R3) and defer (R4–R7) verdicts.

deferral.description: Structured deferral payload — present iff verdict == 'defer'. Closed enum on deferral_reason per AD-2026-04-26X §Q1.

blocking_evidence_id.description: Required iff deferral_reason == 'evidence_incomplete'. The evidence_id whose absence blocks this patch.

retry_after_cycle.description: Optional. Cycle offset at which this patch should be re-evaluated. 1 = next cycle.
